items.title: 'They're often not heard': Hip hop helping kids find words for the feelings

children.content: A Tasmanian youth outreach program is providing free hip hop writing workshops for young people, which experts say could be "life-changing".

items.title: Shyanne Lee-Tatnell's accused murderer likely to face trial next year

children.content: A man who has pleaded not guilty to murdering 14-year-old Tasmanian teenager Shyanne Lee-Tatnell in 2023 will likely stand trial early next year.

image.altText: A young blonde girl smiles at the camera

items.title: Plans for DarkLab Transformer artwork ditched amid rising costs

children.content: The group behind Tasmania's Dark Mofo festival has scrapped plans to develop a "destination public artwork" in the state's south-east due to escalating costs.

image.altText: Mirrored buildings on the side of a hill

items.title: Tougher penalties, but no gun limit under Tasmania's proposed new laws

children.content: New gun laws, which create tougher penalties for the theft and possession of stolen firearms, but do not include gun caps, have been unveiled by the Tasmanian government, asking for public feedback.

gallery.altText: Collection of seized and stolen firearms

thumbnail.altText: Collection of seized and stolen firearms

items.title: Aboriginal activist takes on logging company staff during trespass court case

children.content: An Aboriginal activist has interrogated forestry company staff over their knowledge of cultural heritage as part of her defence against a trespass charge.

image.altText: Ruth Langford, wearing a white jacket, standing in front of the Hobart Magistrates Court smiling

items.title: The ongoing cost of medical misogyny for women

children.content: Australia has come a long way when it comes to women's healthcare, but experts say medical misogyny is still very much alive — and it's not just attitudes that are the problem.

image.altText: A woman lies on the couch with one hand on her head and another on her lower abdomen

items.title: Independent MP David O'Byrne on government's 'messy' six months

children.content: The independent MP calls for greater transparency and says the Tasmanian government needs to reform how ministers rack up legal fees.

items.title: LISTEN: Neil the seal is being used in ad campaigns. Is that ethical?

children.content: On the same day Tasmanians were warned about Neil the seal's safety if visitors keep coming too close, a PR company promoted short-stay accommodation in the location he is currently in.

image.altText: Elephant seal known as Neil the seal, with tongue hanging out, at Seven Mile beach in June 27, 2026.

items.title: Is Tasmania ready for the AI data centre boom?

children.content: An artificial intelligence company wants to build three AI factories in Tasmania, but questions are being raised about the impacts and benefits for the state.

image.altText: A lot in a residential area with a big building on it.

items.title: When a fit young man disappears, he leaves behind a trail of strange clues

children.content: When the first major relationship of Jack McLennan's life implodes, he does what plenty of young men do: meets a mate for a quiet beer. But by morning, Jack's disappeared, leaving behind a series of mysterious clues and a devastated family.

items.title: Billionaires to every day people: Inquiry exposes extent of online hate

children.content: Everyone from billionaires to politicians and pro-Palestinian Jewish activists told the royal commission of the hate they receive in phone calls and emails.

image.altText: composite image of Julie Inman Grant, steven lowy and sarah Schwartz

items.title: 'Towelling the bloke up in the cells': Shocking police emails revealed

items.title: Today Show ratings in 'freefall' as Channel Nine looks to replace Karl Stefanovic

children.content: The Today Show's ratings are in decline following the Nine Network's dumping of host Karl Stefanovic. Former head of News and Current Affairs at Seven, Nine, and 10, Peter Meakin says the audience will want to punish Nine, but things can turn around, even in breakfast TV.

image.altText: Karl Stefanovic in a suit, no tie smiling and standing in front of the Crown

items.title: After 18 months of algal bloom, bird flu has authorities on alert

children.content: While the arrival of bird flu on SA shores is a "huge concern", the state's primary industries minister maintains the government is prepared for the possibility of a wider local outbreak.

image.altText: SA government minister Clare Scriven.

items.title: 175 years ago, Victoria struck gold. 230,000 scars remain

children.content: It has been 175 years since gold was first discovered in Victoria, and now a state government mapping project has discovered that there are far more relics of the gold rush left in the landscape than official records show.
